title: AI Client generation streaming
summary: Generation streaming introduced in the PHP AI Client as an initial effort to unlock full usage in a future release.

title: AI Client embeddings support
summary: Embeddings represent content as vectors to enable meaning-based search across a site.

title: Connectors authentication expansion
summary: Connectors gain more ways to authenticate beyond API keys, starting with username/application password support.

title: Guidelines feature
summary: A new Guidelines feature lets you define writing and content guidelines that tie into AI tooling, with the ability to import/export guidelines between sites.

title: Command palette organization improvements
summary: The command palette now groups results into clear sections for recent, suggested, and matching commands.

title: Admin color scheme in Site Editor
summary: The Site Editor sidebar and overall shell now follow the set WordPress admin color scheme instead of always using a fixed dark background.

title: DataViews and DataForms iterations
summary: Work is underway to migrate DataViews onto the new Design System primitives and consolidate Quick Edit with the editor inspector.

title: Dedicated Identity section
summary: A dedicated Design → Identity screen brings the essentials of your site's identity into one place, with an inline media editor for your logo and favicon.

title: Design System ThemeProvider graduation
summary: A highlight of this cycle is graduating ThemeProvider from experimental to a stable, public API, alongside finalizing the public token names.

title: On This Day dashboard widget
summary: The dashboard is getting a new "On This Day" widget that resurfaces past content, a popular feature across many different platforms.

title: Persistent admin bar (omnibar)
summary: The admin bar is getting some nice polish ahead of being easily accessible in the Site Editor and Block Editor.

title: Revisions improvements
summary: Planned improvements include a spark line view in the scrubbing toolbar to better visualize the history of changes, persistent URLs to allow sharing a link to a particular revision, and more.

title: Abilities API iteration
summary: This cycle advances querying and filtering of abilities and implements a curated set of core abilities (including site settings, current-user info management, and general site awareness).

title: Block Bindings for list items
summary: Block Bindings expands with new support for binding list-item blocks and inner blocks, letting more of your content connect to dynamic data sources.

title: Enforced iframed editor
summary: The current plan is to enforce iframing for block-based themes in this release, then extend it to all themes in a future release.

title: Extended Unicode support
summary: This release is looking to broaden Unicode support so email addresses, usernames, and slugs better reflect WordPress' global audience.

title: React 19 Upgrade
summary: WordPress is upgrading from React 18 to React 19.

title: Icon API expansion
summary: 7.1's iteration centers on opening the API up to third parties with new public functions like register_icon() and unregister_icon(), core-icons theme support, SVG sanitization and namespace validation.

title: Classic block deprecation
summary: The Classic block is planned for deprecation in 7.1, and will no longer appear in the block inserter.

title: Playlist block
summary: Playlist block, with additional waveform audio visualization.

title: Table of Contents block
summary: Table of Contents block, automatically generating navigable links to the headings in your content.

title: Tabs block
summary: Tabs block, organizes content into tabbed panels.

title: Gallery block lightbox refinements
summary: Gallery block lightbox refinements, including swipe indication and opt-in captions on mobile.

title: HTML block new block support
summary: New block support for the HTML block, making it possible to have editable blocks inside of a custom HTML block.

title: Image block decorative toggle
summary: "Mark as decorative" toggle for the Image block to hide decorative images from screen readers for an improved experience.

title: Embed shortcode transform
summary: An [embed] shortcode transform was added to the Embed block, so converting or pasting [embed] shortcodes now creates a proper Embed block.

title: Shortcode block transforms
summary: Block specific transforms were added to the Shortcode block when text matches a registered shortcode.

title: Group block background gradients
summary: The Group block added support for background gradients through a new background.gradient block support, allowing gradients and background images to work together without conflicts.

title: Dialog block
summary: Dialog block for transcripts and conversations.

title: Marquee block
summary: Marquee block for scrolling, animated content.

title: Writing flow improvements
summary: A dedicated focus is on chipping away at everyday pain points in the writing experience, from improving drag and drop to ensuring multi-selection works on touch devices.

title: Notes features expansion
summary: Notes have a range of planned improvements that include notes on specific content within a block and across multiple blocks, rich text in notes, notifications for replies and follows, emoji reactions, a minified notes experience, and an "apply suggestions" feature.

title: Real-time collaboration
summary: Real-time collaboration marches ahead with big, open strategy questions around what to land in this release and what storage mechanism to use.

title: Display inherited styles
summary: This work explores surfacing inherited styles clearly in the sidebar so you can understand where a block's styles are coming from and edit at the right layer of styling.

title: Interactive states styling
summary: Support for pseudo-state styling such as hover, focus, and active has landed for both Global Styles and individual block instances.

title: Pattern editing iterations
summary: For this cycle, work will focus on UX improvements based on feedback around this change, bug fixes, and general maintenance.

title: Responsive styling
summary: This work lets you define how a block looks at different screen sizes, applying responsive styles directly in the editor without writing custom CSS.

title: Viewport breakpoint customization
summary: Theme-configurable breakpoints defined in theme.json are being added to provide more flexible, customizable responsive styling.

title: Client-side media iterations
summary: The work spans HEIC image support, Ultra HDR support, GIF-to-video conversion, more resilient uploads that retry on failure and resume after a crash or going offline, video transcoding to web-safe formats.

title: Media editor modal
summary: The Media editor modal replaces the existing inline cropping tool in the Block Editor, bringing freeform and aspect-ratio cropping, flip, fine-grained and snap rotation, and metadata editing into one dedicated workflow.

title: Media gallery improvements
summary: Galleries are becoming more dynamic and easier to build, with better handling of the legacy gallery shortcode on conversion, dynamic galleries that can sort or pull media attached to a post.

title: Speculative loading update
summary: When both object caching and page caching are detected, the default eagerness would move from conservative to moderate, prefetching and prerendering more readily.

title: View Transitions plugin
summary: Work centers around bringing smooth, animated transitions between pages on the front end.

title: Enhanced Responsive Images plugin
summary: Work computes more accurate sizes values in block themes so browsers download appropriately sized images.
